skill.name: browser-act-skill-forge
skill.description: Explores websites with browser-act and turns verified API, network, DOM, or visual workflows into reusable AI skill packages.
skill.summary: Create reusable browser-act skills from verified website exploration workflows.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found the weak-crypto hits are Markdown and placeholder false positives. The confirmed risks are intentional: the skill generates Python wrappers, invokes shell command substitution, runs browser-side eval, captures network traffic, and writes local skill artifacts. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found, so this is elevated-risk automation rather than a publishing block.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic Browser Eval and Generated Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to generate Python files that print JavaScript and then execute the output through shell command substitution and browser eval. This is expected for the skill purpose, but unsafe generated code or unsafe parameter quoting could execute unintended browser-side JavaScript in an authenticated session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command-substitution and eval workflow is explicit in the instructions. The risk is elevated but partly mitigated because it is a documented, user-directed automation pattern rather than hidden behavior.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Browser Network and DOM Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill directs endpoint discovery, fetch reproduction, DOM extraction, and HAR capture inside the user browser. This can expose or manipulate data available to the logged-in user if applied to sensitive pages or overbroad tasks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The browser network and DOM access is central to the skill and is clearly documented. No evidence shows external exfiltration, but authenticated-session automation is inherently sensitive.

medium_findings.title: User-Authorized Operation Submission Automation
medium_findings.description: The operation workflow captures and packages form submissions and non-GET request behavior. The exploration procedure uses offline HAR capture to reduce side effects, but generated skills may later perform real actions in the user account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly cover form submission and request capture. The safety protocol lowers likelihood of accidental submission during exploration, but runtime side effects remain possible.

low_findings.title: Local Artifact and Memory File Writes
low_findings.description: The skill writes generated skill directories, scripts, temporary artifacts, and optional experience notes. This is expected behavior, but users should choose an output directory carefully and review generated files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-write behavior is explicitly described and bounded to output, tmp, and memory paths. It is a minor risk because it is visible and user-directed.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography detections appear to be false positives from Markdown text, placeholders, filenames, or template content. No cryptographic implementation, digest use, credential hashing, or security decision based on weak algorithms was found in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation templates and examples, not cryptographic code. The static labels do not match the semantic content at the cited locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution Around Generated Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: Command templates use eval with shell command substitution to execute JavaScript emitted by generated Python scripts. Generated scripts and parameter quoting should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in command templates and verification steps. It is dangerous by capability, even though it is part of the advertised workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser-Side Fetch and Network Capture
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to reproduce site requests with fetch and inspect network requests or HAR files. This can reveal sensitive account-scoped data when used on authenticated sites.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fetch and network-capture instructions are explicit. The risk depends on the target site and user authorization, so it is not evidence of malware by itself.

content.value_statement: Website automation often fails when agents repeat fragile exploration steps. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code turn verified browser-act workflows into reusable skill packages.
actual_capabilities: Guides browser-act exploration for extraction and operation workflows.
Prioritizes internal API discovery, then network capture, DOM extraction, and AI visual workflows.
Defines generated skill structure with SKILL.md files and Python wrapper scripts.
Includes verification steps for generated scripts before installation or batch use.
Documents pagination, enum parameter collection, limitations, and execution notes for generated skills.
limitations: Requires browser-act availability and access to the target page in the user browser.
Generated automations inherit the user account permissions and site restrictions.
Dynamic pages, CAPTCHA, and anti-automation controls may require manual or visual steps.
The source skill is procedural documentation and does not include a standalone runtime library.

use_cases.title: Build a Repeatable Data Extractor
use_cases.description: Turn a verified website search, list, or detail-page workflow into a reusable skill for repeated extraction.

use_cases.title: Package Internal Web Operations
use_cases.description: Capture a safe browser workflow for forms or account actions that a user can already perform manually.

use_cases.title: Investigate Frontend API Behavior
use_cases.description: Explore which network requests, parameters, and pagination patterns power a target website feature.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Extraction Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one target site and one data type.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable browser-act skill for extracting {data type} from {website URL}. Confirm the plan before exploration.

prompt_templates.title: Find the API Behind a Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on endpoint discovery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explore {website URL} and identify the endpoint or DOM path used to load {data}. Package the verified method as a skill.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Batch Workflow Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for repeated extraction at scale.
prompt_templates.prompt: Forge a browser-act skill that can collect {records} across pagination and filters on {site}. Include verification and batch execution guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Operation Automation Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a user-authorized browser operation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable skill for performing {operation} on {site} within my authenticated browser session. Use safety verification before packaging it.

output_examples.input: Create a skill to extract product names and prices from a marketplace search page.
output: A generated skill directory with instructions and script wrappers.
A verified extraction approach using API, network capture, DOM, or visual workflow.
Notes about pagination, missing fields, and account or site limits.

output_examples.input: Find what endpoint loads issue lists in a web dashboard and make it reusable.
output: A documented endpoint or fallback workflow with parameter guidance.
A reusable command pattern for future issue-list extraction.
Verification results showing that returned data matches the page.

output_examples.input: Package a browser workflow for submitting an internal form.
output: A generated operation skill with prerequisites and pre-execution checks.
A safe verification record based on captured request intent.
Clear limitations for login state, permissions, and dynamic controls.

best_practices: Confirm the target site, objective, output path, and execution intent before exploration.
Verify generated scripts on small test cases before batch execution.
Document account permissions, pagination limits, and any fields that are not fully covered.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to bypass authentication, paywalls, or access controls.
Do not run generated scripts against sensitive pages without reviewing the workflow.
Do not start large batch runs before validating one or two representative cases.

faq.question: Does this skill scrape websites directly?
faq.answer: It guides browser-act exploration and packages verified workflows. The generated skills operate through the user browser session.

faq.question: Can it work on logged-in sites?
faq.answer: Yes, when the user can already access the data or action manually in the browser.

faq.question: Does it send data to outside services?
faq.answer: The source instructions state that artifacts stay local and traffic goes to the target site. Generated skills should still be reviewed.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It requires browser-act and an AI tool that can read the skill instructions, such as Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it automate form submissions?
faq.answer: Yes. That capability is powerful and should be limited to actions the user explicitly authorizes.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: It generates scripts, uses shell command substitution, and runs browser JavaScript in authenticated sessions, but no malicious intent was found.
